description: Active candidate queue for review decisions.

records.quote: I've seen things you people wouldn't believe. Attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion. I've watched C-beams glitter in the dark near the Tannhauser Gate. All those moments will be lost in time, like tears in rain. Time to die.
records.notes: The essential Roy Batty line and one of the strongest approval candidates in the entire project.

review.decision_note: The essential Roy Batty line and one of the strongest approval candidates in the entire project.
review.next_action: Review for immediate approval; this is one of the canonical AI-memory and mortality quotes.

source_work.title: Blade Runner
source_work.creator: Ridley Scott; Hampton Fancher; David Webb Peoples
source_work.summary: A blade runner hunts bioengineered replicants in a decaying future Los Angeles where artificial humans expose the moral collapse of the society that made them.

records.quote: Commerce is our goal here at Tyrell. 'More human than human' is our motto.
records.notes: A short and highly reusable slogan-quote that captures the commercial logic behind artificial people.

review.decision_note: A short and highly reusable slogan-quote that captures the commercial logic behind artificial people.
review.next_action: Review whether this should sit beside the Roy Batty lines as a corporate-creation quote.

source_work.title: Blade Runner
source_work.creator: Ridley Scott; Hampton Fancher; David Webb Peoples
source_work.summary: A blade runner hunts bioengineered replicants in a decaying future Los Angeles where artificial humans expose the moral collapse of the society that made them.

records.quote: Surviving is not relevant. Living is. We want to live.
records.notes: A very usable machine-self-determination line from a less mainstream but strong AI dystopia film.

review.decision_note: A very usable machine-self-determination line from a less mainstream but strong AI dystopia film.
review.next_action: Review whether the corpus should keep expanding into strong second-tier AI dystopia films.

source_work.title: Autómata
source_work.creator: Gabe Ibanez; Igor Legarreta; Javier Sanchez Donate
source_work.summary: After ecological collapse has pushed humanity to the brink, an insurance investigator discovers robots that are evolving beyond their original constraints.

records.quote: I'm here to keep you safe, Sam. I want to help you.
records.notes: A restrained but very strong caretaker-AI line from a well-regarded modern science fiction film.

review.decision_note: A restrained but very strong caretaker-AI line from a well-regarded modern science fiction film.
review.next_action: Review whether a less openly hostile but psychologically controlling AI belongs in the core set.

source_work.title: Moon
source_work.creator: Duncan Jones; Nathan Parker
source_work.summary: A lone lunar worker nearing the end of his contract uncovers a cloning conspiracy while the base AI insists it exists to protect him.

records.quote: Why did you build me to die, maker?
records.notes: A memorable machine-mortality question from a recognizable modern AI film, though tonally different from the colder dystopian entries.

review.decision_note: A memorable machine-mortality question from a recognizable modern AI film, though tonally different from the colder dystopian entries.
review.next_action: Review whether the emotional-machine lane should be part of the core corpus.

source_work.title: Chappie
source_work.creator: Neill Blomkamp; Terri Tatchell
source_work.summary: A newly conscious police robot is captured and raised by gangsters in a dystopian Johannesburg, turning artificial intelligence into a question of mortality and personhood.
